blurb: The Father's Prayer reveals the radical intimacy of the Stranger God—not a distant Creator to be approached with elaborate ritual, but a loving Parent who delights in simple communion with his children. Where the Demiurge's demands require perfect compliance before blessing, the Father invites us to ask freely, knowing that his grace precedes our prayers and his provision flows from love rather than merit. This prayer teaches us to approach heaven not as subjects seeking favor from a judge, but as children confident in their Father's tender care.

verses.text: And it came to pass that, as he was praying in a certain place, when he ceased, one of his disciples said unto him, Lord, teach us to pray, as John also taught his disciples.
And he said unto them, When ye pray, say,
